task_completion.description: The function f(x) = x^3 - 3x + 1 is plotted in the visualization.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A cubic curve is visible in the graphing area, and the algebraic expression f(x) = x^3 - 3x + 1 is listed in the GeoGebra algebra panel.

task_completion.description: The derivative function f'(x) is plotted alongside the original function.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A parabola (representing the derivative 3x^2 - 3) is visible in the graphing area, and its expression is listed in the algebra panel.

task_completion.description: An interactive tangent line is attached to a moveable point on the curve f(x).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A straight line is drawn tangent to the cubic curve. The algebra panel should show a point defined on the curve f(x) and a line defined using the Tangent command or tool.

task_completion.description: The critical points where f'(x) = 0 are explicitly identified on the graph.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Points are explicitly marked at the local maximum (-1, 3) and local minimum (1, -1) of the cubic curve, or at the roots of the parabola (-1, 0) and (1, 0). These points should be visible in the algebra panel.

task_completion.description: A display of numerical values showing the instantaneous rate of change at the moveable point.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dynamic text or a value on the screen showing the slope of the tangent line (e.g., 'm = ...') or the evaluated derivative at the current location of the moveable point.

task_completion.description: The file is saved as 'derivative_explorer.ggb' in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra window title shows 'derivative_explorer.ggb', a save dialog shows this filename, or a terminal/file manager window shows the file located in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to create the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GeoGebra application open with the graph and algebra panels actively being used, rather than a text editor writing a .ggb (ZIP/XML) file directly.

integrity.description: The mathematical elements are genuinely constructed in GeoGebra.
integrity.visual_evidence: The algebra panel shows proper GeoGebra commands and objects (e.g., functions, points, tangents) rather than just static text boxes or disconnected line segments placed to look like the answer.

integrity.description: The tangent line and rate of change are dynamically linked to the function, not hardcoded.
integrity.visual_evidence: The tangent line is defined using a point on the object and the Tangent tool/command, rather than a static line equation typed manually for a single specific coordinate.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The function to be plotted is f(x) = x^3 - 3x + 1. Its derivative is f'(x) = 3x^2 - 3 (which graphs as a parabola). The critical points occur exactly at x = -1 (local maximum at y = 3) and x = 1 (local minimum at y = -1). The expected output filename is 'derivative_explorer.ggb'.
